mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models, but may require careful resource management and tuning for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to a directory in your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command fetches the credentials for your cluster and updates your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d application using Helm, which simplifies deployment and management.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Adds the llm-d Helm repository and updates your local repo cache.
> Installs the llm-d application in a new namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/examples/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/examples/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d application and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

kubectl delete namespace llm-d

```

> This command removes the llm-d release from your cluster.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it, which may result in data loss.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up your configuration, upgrading the application, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backs up the current configuration.
> Upgrades the llm-d application to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required resources.

**Fix:**
Check the application logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and verify the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster or adjust resource requests in the deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the service status with 'kubectl get services -n llm-d' and check the pod logs.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new accelerators and improved autoscaling features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.28.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d
helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
